title: Disentangling ERBB signaling in breast cancer subtypes - a model-based analysis
author: Wiemann, Stefan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Human breast cancer cell lines MCF-7 (HTB-22), T47D (HTB-133), MDA-MB-231 (HTB-26) and SKBR3 (HTB-30) were obtained from the American Type Culture Collection (ATCC, LGC Standards GmbH, Wesel, Germany) and maintained in Dulbecco’s Modified Eagle Medium: Nutrient Mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12 without phenol red, supplemented with 10% FCS, 50 units/ml penicillin and 50 µg/ml streptomycin sulfate) at 37°C with 5% CO2. All cell lines were authenticated (Multiplexion, Heidelberg, Germany) and negatively tested for mycoplasma contamination before and after completion of the study. 
3x10e5 cells (MCF7, T47D, and MDA-MB-231) were seeded in 6-well plates and starved overnight in DMEM/F12 without FCS. Then (t=0), drugs (cetuximab, trastuzumab, pertuzumab, lumretuzumab, or erlotinib – Drugs.csv) were applied for 1 hour. Samples were taken at 0, 5, 10 and 60 minutes after incubation with respective drugs (t=0, 5, 10, 60 min). Then, signaling was stimulated with different growth factors (EGF, TGF-alpha, betacellulin or neuregulin – GrowthFactors.csv). Samples were taken at several time points after addition of drugs and for up to 1500 minutes after addition of growth factors (t=65, 70, 80, 100, 150, 240, 360, 1020, 1560 min). Every condition was tested in three independent biological replicates. Lysates were collected with mammalian protein extraction reagent (M-PERTM) containing protease inhibitor CompleteMini and phosphatase inhibitor PhosSTOP (Both Roche). 
3x10e5 cells (SKBR3) were seeded in 6-well plates and starved overnight in DMEM/F12 without FCS. Then (t=0 min), drugs (trastuzumab, pertuzumab, or lumretuzumab – Drugs.csv) were applied for 1 hour. Samples were taken at 0 and 60 minutes after incubation with respective drugs (t=0, 60 min). Then, signaling was stimulated with different growth factors (EGF or neuregulin – GrowthFactors.csv). Samples were taken at several time points after addition of drugs and for up to 90 minutes after addition of growth factors (t=65, 80, 150 min). Every condition was tested in three independent biological replicates. Lysates were collected with mammalian protein extraction reagent (M-PERTM) containing protease inhibitor CompleteMini and phosphatase inhibitor PhosSTOP (Both Roche). 
Protein concentration in lysates was adjusted to 2 µg/µL, and lysates spotted on nitrocellulose-coated glass slides (Oncocyte Avid, Grace Biolabs) with three technical replicates of every biological replicate. Every 10th slide was processed without antibodies but incubated with Fast green FCF stain for quantification of total protein levels. Other arrays were processed with primary antibodies (File:PrimaryAntibodies.csv) to tag proteins and phosphorylated proteins (incubation over night at 4°C). Unbound antibodies were washed off and antibodies bound to the array then tagged with matching secondary antibodies (File:SecondaryAntibodies.csv) that were labeled with Alexa Fluor 680. 
Images of slides were obtained (Odyssey scanner, LiCor) at an excitation wavelength of 685 nm and a resolution of 150 μm using the Odyssey Scanner (LI-COR). Signal intensities of each individual spot were quantified using GenePixPro 5.0 (Molecular Devices). Preprocessing and scaling of the RPPA data, background correction, normalization (FCF staining of total proteins), and merging of technical triplicates was performed in R using the software packages RPPanalyzer and BlotIt2. Datasets are provided in individual files (Data-MDA-MB-231.csv, Data-T47D.csv, Data-MCF7.csv, Data-SKBR3.csv).

Human breast cancer cell lines MCF-7 (HTB-22), T47D (HTB-133), MDA-MB-231 (HTB-26) and SKBR3 (HTB-30) were obtained from the American Type Culture Collection (ATCC, LGC Standards GmbH, Wesel, Germany) and maintained in Dulbecco’s Modified Eagle Medium: Nutrient Mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12 without phenol red, supplemented with 10% FCS, 50 units/ml penicillin and 50 µg/ml streptomycin sulfate) at 37°C with 5% CO2.
